title: Autumn foliage in Langhe's vineyards #shorts #travel #grinzanecavour #langhe #drone #foliage
creator: Wander Peaks (YouTube channel: @WanderPeaks)
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (autumn and seasonal-foliage vineyard landscapes) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusting the prior agent's candidate JSON verbatim) via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/p23-HUg8wak&format=json), which returned title="Autumn foliage in Langhe's vineyards #shorts #travel #grinzanecavour #langhe #drone #foliage", author_name="Wander Peaks", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@WanderPeaks", provider_name="YouTube" -- matching the candidate record's title/creator field-for-field, so this session's own primary-source check confirms rather than merely restates the prior agent's claim. A second WebFetch of the live Shorts page itself (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/p23-HUg8wak) was made specifically to check for a Creative Commons marking; it returned no mention of "Creative Commons", "CC BY", or any license-attribution notice anywhere in the fetched content (the response consisted mainly of generic YouTube footer/navigation text, i.e. it did not surface the page's actual license widget, but no CC marking of any kind was found). A third WebFetch of the channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@WanderPeaks) was attempted to look for subscriber count, channel description, or stated drone equipment/resolution, but returned only a truncated stub ("Wander Peaks - YouTube [Content truncated due to length...]") with no extractable subscriber count, bio, or equipment information -- this is disclosed rather than papered over. A WebSearch this session ("Wander Peaks" YouTube channel drone travel subscribers) returned no results attributable to this specific channel at all -- only unrelated travel-channel results (Wander Z Vlogs, Wend + Wander, The Wandering Traveller, etc.) -- so no independent secondary-source corroboration of this creator's reach, equipment, or production quality was found in this session. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because no explicit Creative Commons marking was confirmed on this specific upload (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field for this URL, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session; the URL path itself (/shorts/) structurally implies a sub-60s runtime by platform definition, but the exact cut point is unverified, so rather than fabricate a plausible number this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual/small travel-drone channel's hashtag-driven Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. These are calibrated at the low end of this pack's documented 0.72-0.88 band, at or slightly below the pack's other unvetted-personal-creator exemplar (instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json, 0.74/0.73/0.72): unlike that exemplar, no drone model, resolution, or export setting is disclosed anywhere in this video's title, hashtags (#shorts #travel #grinzanecavour #langhe #drone #foliage -- generic, no model-specific tag comparable to that record's #mavicair2s), or the (truncated) channel page, and no follower/engagement signal could be obtained via WebSearch either -- so there is no positive technical- baseline signal at all to lift these above the floor, only the generic observation that this is professionally-framed-looking rapid aerial-pass travel content typical of the genre. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (resolved to weights_version='default-v2', 0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition) via `.venv/bin/python3`, returning composite_score=0.7233333333333333 (rounded to 0.7233 here). Because `composition` is `None`, `compute_composite_score` skips the composition term entirely (its weight-or-score-is-None guard), so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals. `editorial_style.format` is set to 'short-form-reel' on a structural basis -- the source URL itself is a /shorts/ path and the oEmbed title includes the literal '#shorts' hashtag, both platform-format facts rather than a playback-derived judgment. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (browser automation tools were available but not invoked, as doing so would require an interactive browser-selection step out of scope for this bounded verification task), so `estimated_cut_count`, `avg_shot_length_seconds`, and `transition_styles_observed` are left null/empty rather than guessed -- no WebFetch or WebSearch call in this session surfaced any text describing cut rhythm, shot count, transition style, or pacing for this specific video, so per the schema's honesty contract those fields stay unset; `review_method` is set to 'text_provenance_only' to reflect that the one populated field (`format`) came from URL/title structure, not actual viewing.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in this session's own observation that the source_url is a /shorts/ path and the oEmbed-returned title contains the literal '#shorts' hashtag -- both structural/platform facts, not a playback-derived judgment. No live playback or scrubbing was attempted in this session, and no WebFetch/WebSearch call surfaced cut-rhythm, shot-count, transition, or pacing text for this specific video, so estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed.
